title: SQL Server Stale Statistics Investigation
description: Identify stale statistics causing bad cardinality estimates, wrong join strategies, and query degradation
author: Q. Miller

stats_staleness_scan.description: Statistics staleness scan — tables with high modification counters and old stats

interactive_hints.cardinality_estimate_errors: Run to see which active queries have the largest gap between estimated and actual row counts — directly links stale stats to query impact
interactive_hints.stats_histogram_detail: Run to inspect the actual histogram of the most stale statistics object — shows the data skew causing the bad estimate
interactive_hints.stop: Stop here and generate the final analysis with results gathered so far

cardinality_estimate_errors.description: Active queries with largest estimated vs actual row count divergence

interactive_hints.stats_histogram_detail: Run to inspect the histogram of the statistics driving the worst cardinality error — confirms the exact data distribution problem and the date boundary where the histogram falls off
interactive_hints.stop: Stop here if the cardinality error data is sufficient for your recommendation
interactive_hints.switch_to_sqlserver-plan-regression: Switch to the Plan Regression playbook to check whether Query Store has already captured forced regressions for these queries — you likely need both plan forcing (immediate relief) and statistics rebuild (permanent fix) in parallel

stats_histogram_detail.description: Histogram inspection for the most impactful stale statistics object

interactive_hints.stop: Stop here and generate the final analysis with update statistics recommendations
interactive_hints.switch_to_sqlserver-plan-regression: Switch to the Plan Regression playbook to force the last known good plan while the statistics rebuild runs — the forced plan provides immediate query relief and can be removed once the histogram is current
